page: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Edsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided via a 118 GB local disk, and the server is premium storage capable. No GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 2215.58, though multi-core performance is weak at 2263.98 due to the 2-vCPU limit. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with large-block write speeds at 12.15 GB/sec. However, the system maintains average performance in PassMark memory tests and small-model LLM text generation. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive multi-core processing.

This server is suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and light analytical workloads that benefit from a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio but do not demand high parallel processing power.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 2 vCPUs (1 physical core) running at 4.2 GHz, backed by a 480 MB L3 cache and advanced instructions like AMX and AVX-512. The instance features 16.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, alongside 118 GB of local storage. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU and memory performance, but weak multi-core and memory write bandwidth. This resource profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, low-compute tasks. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and local storage for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

16.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
118 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Best suited for small databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
